steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The archaeologists' assumption is: a major demographic shift causes simultaneous reductions in multiple specialized crafting traditions. The evidence shows that around 1500 BCE, gold filigree declined steeply—but advanced copper-alloy metallurgy and most other specialized traditions continued to flourish. The blank must draw an inference consistent with both the assumption and the evidence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — disruptions around 1500 BCE in the utilization of gold filigree likely occurred in the context of demographic continuity among peoples of the Alazani River valley.: only one tradition declined while others flourished. Under the assumption, a demographic shift would have caused simultaneous declines in multiple traditions—so the absence of that pattern points to demographic continuity. ✓
  • Choice B — a sudden simultaneous decline in artifacts from multiple specialized crafting traditions is less likely than previously assumed to indicate that the transmission of expertise was disrupted by demographic changes.: this directly contradicts the assumption stated in the passage, which holds that simultaneous declines do indicate demographic changes. ✗
  • Choice C — peoples in the Alazani River valley continued to uphold multiple specialized crafting traditions, including goldsmithing and copper-alloy metallurgy, even as demographic shifts occurred.: the evidence shows gold filigree declined, so goldsmithing was not upheld. Also, asserting demographic shifts occurred contradicts what the evidence implies under the assumption. ✗
  • Choice D — cross-cultural transmission between distinct demographic groups in the Alazani River valley likely explains the expansion of copper-alloy metallurgy beginning around 1500 BCE.: the passage does not mention expansion of copper-alloy metallurgy, only that it continued. This introduces an explanation not supported by the data. ✗
That matches Choice A — disruptions around 1500 BCE in the utilization of gold filigree likely occurred in the context of demographic continuity among peoples of the Alazani River valley..
